title: /! command passes raw user input to sh -c
description: tokio::process::Command::new('sh').arg('-c').arg(cmd_str) where cmd_str is raw user input. Shell metacharacters are interpreted: /! echo $HOME expands env vars, /! cmd1; cmd2 runs both commands, /! $(whoami) executes subshell. Output is sent to the chat room, visible to all peers. User expects /! to run 'a command', not 'a shell expression'. A user typing /! cat notes.txt | grep password and thinking only grep output is sent will be surprised when the whole pipeline runs and the relay fans the output to all N peers. Maintenance consequence: class of user-reported 'unexpected behavior' issues whenever shell syntax appears in commands.
close_reason: Replaced sh -c with shlex word-splitting + Command::new(argv[0]).args(argv[1..]). Shell metacharacters are no longer interpreted. Added usage error for empty/invalid parse. Updated /help text.
